sutta: and 40 1 monks these 5 persons are found living in the world what 5 1 who gives and despises a man 1 who despises a man by living with him or lives with another and despises him 1 who has a mouth to take in anything 1 who wavers and 1 who is foolish and mind tossed and how monks does a person give and despise a man here in monks a person to a person gives requisites the rope arms lodging and medicines and things i give this fellow receives he gives and despises him thus monks a person gives and despises a man and how monks does a person despise a man by living with him here in monks a person lives with a person for 2 or 3 years by living with him he despises him thus monks a person despises a man by living with him and how monks has a person a mouth to take in anything here in monks a person while another is being spoken of in praise or blame just promptly revels in it thus monks a person has a mouth to take in anything and how monks does a person waver here in monks a person is uncertain in faith uncertain in devotion uncertain in affection uncertain in goodness thus amongst a person wavers and how monks is a person foolish and mind tossed here in monks a person does not know good conditions from bad does not know blame worthy conditions from blameless does not know low conditions from lofty does not know whether conditions are evenly mixed with bright and dark qualities thus monks a person is foolish and mind tossed verily monks these 5 persons are found living in the world it's the end of the suta
commentary:
here the first 1 the buddha is talking about kind of person who gives to another and because of that despises the other fellow like some people they don't have much respect for monks sometimes they give and um like it he throws some things and i'm not giving to a beggar and some people they think monks receive all the time and they they uh because of that they don't like monks some people they give and then they lose respect the second 1 by living with another person a person looks at all his faults and learns to despise him he forgets that he himself has false we all have thoughts it's very easy to look at another person's fault and the third 1 he has a mouth to take in anything while another person is being spoken of in praise or blame just probably revels in it i suppose it is a type of person who is a gullible person whatever he hears he accepts without investigating and then the fourth person is a person who wavers whatever he does he has a lot of a lot of doubts and doubts is having doubts his dogs is 1 of the 5 hindrances that prevent us from seeing things as they really are mind is unsteady and the only way we can overcome this doubt is to have strong samadhi when the mind is very strong and then whatever problem comes up we can examine it very clearly then we won't have doubt and the last person is a person who is foolish and mind tossed he cannot differentiate between good and bad conditions from blameworthy and blameless conditions as a foolish person these are the different kinds of person found in the world the next few times 5.15.100 and 40 3 once while he dwelt near vesali at the gabled hall in mahavana he exalted 1 early 1 morning after dressing took bowl and rope and entered way sally for arms now at that time about 500 leechevies were meeting and seated at sarandar shrine and this talk occurred rarely are the 5 treasures revealed in the world what 5 rarely is the elephant treasure revealed in the world rarely the horse treasure the jewel treasure the woman treasure and rarely the householder treasure rarely are these 5 treasures revealed in the world i'll just stop here for a moment these people are talking about the 5 rare things that appear in the world when a real turning king a universal monarch appears in the world this universal mana is a king who has such so much blessings and merit that he is able to rule the whole world and when he appears these treasures also appear and he owns them how to continue the sutta now these literavies had put a man in the road saying when my man you see the exalted 1 coming along come and tell us and the man saw the exalted 1 a good way of coming along and he went and told the lichavi saying sirth here comes the same exalted 1 and now is the time to do what you meant and the lichavis approached the exalted 1 saluted him and stood at 1 side and so standing they said lord it were good if the exalted 1 were to visit sarandar dash shrine out of compassion the exalted 1 accepted in silence and when he had come to the shrine he sat down on the seat they had made ready and so seated he said this to the richavis what did you talk of or literally as you said here together just now what talk between you was interrupted lord as we said together the talk turned on how rarely the 5 treasures are revealed in the world what 5 rallies the elephant treasure reveals in the world rarely the horse treasure the jewel treasure the woman treasure the householder treasure rarely are these 5 treasures revealed in the world and the buddha said truly with you lichvis sense and thrall talk between you turns to just 2 things of the flesh 5 holichovis are the treasures rarely revealed in the world what 5 the tathagata arahan buddha is rarely revealed in the world where in the world is a person able to teach dhamma vinaya declared by the tatar gatherer where is a person able to appreciate the teaching rare in the world is a person who conducts himself in dhamma by dhamma recognizing the teaching dharmavinaya and declared by the tatar gather rare in the world is a person who is grateful and thankful verily all ichabees these are the 5 treasures rarely revealed in the world that's the end of the sutta here the buddha declares a different set of 5 persons rarely found in the world the first 1 is a buddha i think remember i i mentioned that the buddha said that he looked into the past world cycles and he saw in the past 91 world cycles only 6 buddhas appeared which means it takes more on on the average of more than 10 past 10 world cycles for a buddha to to appear that's why the buddha is very rare the 1 who teaches the dhamma the second 1 is a person able to teach dhamma vinaya according to the way like the buddha teaches according to the meaning taught by the buddha [Applause] and the third 1 is a person who is able to appreciate the teaching and 1 who appreciates the dhamma taught by the buddha see like tonight we have this talk and not many people here only about 20 people yeah kl there's so many people and so many buddhists but not many people really appreciate the dhamma some people find the dhamma very dry but when you understand the dhamma then you learn then you know how good is the dhamma and the fourth 1 rare in the world is a person who conducts himself in dharma by dhamma that means who lives his life according to dhamma and that can only be a person who understands the dhamma and that is quite rare and the last 1 is a person who is grateful and thankful ever every so often we come across ungrateful people people whom we have done a service to and they don't appreciate and don't show gratitude but that's the way the world is so now we have to accept that now
